settings.faq.title: FAQ
settings.faq.description: Practical answers for pricing, dictation metrics, notes behavior, runtime modes, and paste workflows.
settings.faq.q1: How are costs calculated in this app?
settings.faq.a1: Whispy uses token counts from your content and model prices from the LiteLLM pricing catalog. Dictation and notes values are estimates, but notes spend counts only processed Enhanced output.
settings.faq.q2: Why can estimates differ from provider billing?
settings.faq.a2: Providers can tokenize text differently and some transcription models bill by audio minute. Whispy keeps practical estimates for visibility and marks pricing source status clearly.
settings.faq.q3: What does "N/A time" mean in Dictations?
settings.faq.a3: Older history entries may not include recording duration metadata. In that case, entry-level time is shown as N/A, while aggregate time can still use a words-per-minute fallback estimate.
settings.faq.q4: What does Recorded time mean in Dictations stats?
settings.faq.a4: It is total captured dictation duration. If a session has no explicit duration, Whispy estimates from transcript length to keep totals readable.
settings.faq.q5: How should I read words and tokens in Dictations?
settings.faq.a5: Words are human-readable transcript length. Tokens are model-oriented units used for pricing estimates. The line format is: language | provider/model | time | words | tokens | estimated cost.
settings.faq.q6: How should I read Notes stats?
settings.faq.a6: Notes stats are focused on Enhanced output: how many notes are processed, how many enhanced words/tokens were produced, and how many notes are still draft-only.
settings.faq.q7: Why does Notes estimate ignore draft-only notes?
settings.faq.a7: To avoid misleading totals, draft notes are not counted as spent usage. Only notes with Enhanced output are included in notes spend and overall spend.
settings.faq.a8: Yes. In Settings > Models > Post-processing toggle, switch it off. Dictations will return raw transcript text, and note enhancement actions are bypassed until you enable it again.
settings.faq.q9: Why do I see N/A in Notes stats when post-processing is disabled?
settings.faq.a9: When post-processing is disabled, new enhancement spend is marked as N/A by design. Historical enhanced spend is still shown separately so your budget history remains visible.
settings.faq.a10: Overall combines dictation estimate plus notes enhanced spend currently tracked in your app state. It is intended for practical monitoring, not invoice-level reconciliation.
settings.faq.q11: What do pricing source colors mean?
settings.faq.a11: Green means live pricing data, yellow means cached pricing data, red means pricing source unavailable. If unavailable, values may be partial or fallback-based.
settings.faq.q12: How often is pricing data refreshed?
settings.faq.a12: Pricing data is cached and reused for performance, then refreshed on demand (Refresh button) or when cache expires. This prevents noisy network calls and improves responsiveness.
settings.faq.q13: Which paste shortcut should I use?
settings.faq.a13: Use Ctrl+V for standard editors. Use Ctrl+Shift+V for terminals and apps that expect plain-text paste. You can switch this in Preferences at any time.
settings.faq.q14: How do I set up ydotool if auto-paste fails?
settings.faq.a14: Whispy tries to start ydotoold automatically. If permissions are blocked, run ydotoold with a user-owned socket and set YDOTOOL_SOCKET before launching Whispy. Example: ydotoold --socket-path="$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/.ydotool_socket" --socket-perm=0600 & then export YDOTOOL_SOCKET="$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/.ydotool_socket".
settings.faq.a15: Local models are loaded only when local runtime is active. Switching back to cloud stops local server processes so RAM/VRAM is released after process shutdown.
settings.faq.q16: Why can model scan fail for some providers?
settings.faq.a16: Some providers do not expose a standard models listing endpoint for your key or plan. Whispy handles these gracefully and avoids repeated noisy scan errors where possible.
settings.faq.a17: Spending Limits are soft limits per provider for awareness and budget planning. They currently do not hard-block API traffic automatically.
settings.faq.a18: Settings and history are stored in app data; notes are stored as markdown files with metadata indexes. Secret values are handled via keyring/.env fallback based on platform support.
settings.debugLogs.title: Debug logs
settings.debugLogs.description: Quick debug controls and log locations.

settings.debugLogs.keyringFallbackWarnin: Keyring fallback is active. API keys are currently served from plaintext `.env` until keyring access is restored.
